feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Data Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Data Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is Hyphen supported for Server Name in SAP Data Services?
entry.content:
Using a hyphen (-) in the hostname is fully supported for DS/IPS installation and operation?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: is SAP BODS capable to write to Microsoft Fabric Warehouse?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
I'm trying to write to Microsoft Fabric with SAP BODS.

I can not create a working BODS Datastore with Fabric (datastore type: database, database type: Microsoft).

I can create an ODBC based datastore and for data reading it works.

when I try to write to MS Fabric warehouse, BODS is haging during the write, the problem seems to be on the commit as the files under the delta tables are created.

Have you experienced this? (BODS 4.2.14, odbc driver for SQL server version 17).

Thanks,
Alberto

entry.title: SAP Data Services, generated ABAP DF program can not be transported with variables!!
entry.content:
hello experts,

I try to generate an ABAP program related to an ABAP Dataflow, the DF uses a global variable (or a parameter) => Upload Ok.

I try to release the CR in SAP S/4HANA 2021 system => CR releasae Ok.

When I transport there are errors related to how the Global variables or parameters in ABAP dataflow are translated in PARAMETER $PARAM# in ABAP program (# = 1,2,3... positional parameter identifier).

So during STMS transport of a generated ABAP program from an ABAP dataflow, a check on ABAP consistency is made (parameters needs to start with literals or '_').
By default the code generated by SAP DS contains the '$' symbol as first character.

This prevent of transporting any ABAP dataflow that is using Gobal variables or Parameters.

There is no workaround or mention in SAP Help (tested an export of PDF and query to notebooklm),

I can't find any workaround in SAP notes.

 

This limitation prevent the usage of "Execute Preloaded" with SAP S/4 or SAP ECC production datastore, as the ABAP programs can not be transported beccause of '$' issue, and with closed client we can not directly or manually upload these programs to S/4HANA productive tenant.

So the only workaround I found is open the client to changes and use "Generate and Execute" with a productive S/4HANA system.

 

Do you have any workaround?

best regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Clarification on Row Type "NORMAL" behavior
entry.content:
I am currently studying SAP Data Services (BODS) and have a question regarding how the Operation Code (Row Type) changes throughout a Dataflow. Specifically, I am confused about the different behaviors of the "NORMAL" flag depending on whether a Table_Comparison (TC) transform is used.

Based on the official documentation, I understand that:

  1. All rows extracted from a source are initially flagged as NORMAL.

  2. When a row flagged as NORMAL is loaded into a target, it is treated as a new row and an INSERT operation is performed.

I have designed a test Dataflow as shown in the attached image to compare two scenarios where the source and target data are identical (no changes).

 

Scenario 1: Source -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP2)

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: It triggers a Unique Constraint Violation error because the NORMAL flag from the source is treated as an INSERT. I understand this part as it aligns with the documentation.

Scenario 2: Source -> Table_Comparison (TC) -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP)

  • TC Setting: Comparing Source and Target.

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: The rows are Discarded (no SQL is executed) by the target.

My Questions: In Scenario 2, how is each row flagged at each stage of the flow when the source and target data match exactly?

  • Source Table Stage: Is the row flagged as NORMAL?

  • Table_Comparison Stage: Does the TC transform maintain the NORMAL flag because it's a "Match with no difference"?

  • Map_Operation Stage: If the MO receives a NORMAL flag and outputs it as NORMAL, why does the Target treat this differently compared to Scenario 1?

Does the Table_Comparison transform add some internal metadata or "instruction" to the NORMAL flag that tells the Target to discard it instead of inserting it? I would appreciate a clear explanation of how the engine distinguishes between these two "Normal" states.

 

Here is the screenshot. 

 

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Installation SAP Data Services 4.3
entry.content:
I would like to install SAP Data Services 4.3, and I want to make sure I fully understand the prerequisites before getting started.
So far, I know that I need to:

Download and install the SAP Installation Planner (IPS) to generate the installation files.

Install the Data Services 4.3 Server on the target server.

Install the Data Services 4.3 Client (Designer) on the user workstations.

However, I am confused about the CMS (Central Management Server). I’m not exactly sure what it is:

  1. Is it a standalone product?
  2. Is it part of IPS or the Data Services Server/Client?
  3. Is it mandatory to use SAP Data Services, or only required if one wants to integrate with the SAP BusinessObjects Management Console?

I would like to clarify the role of the CMS in the SAP Data Services 4.3 architecture and understand whether I can install and use DS 4.3 independently for my ETL jobs and database connections without installing the CMS.

Thank you in advance for your clarifications!

entry.title: Unable to Create Custom Analytical Query in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Good afternoon,

I’m reaching out to ask for some guidance regarding an issue I’m experiencing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

I’m trying to create a new Custom Analytical Query using the Custom Analytical Queries app in the test environment, as I would like to start exploring and working with this functionality. However, when I access the app, the “Create” button is disabled, and I’m unable to proceed.

I have already reviewed the business roles and catalog permissions assigned to my user, and they appear to include the necessary authorizations to create analytical queries.

These are my business roles

These are permissions for business rol 1

These are permissions for business rol 2

I’ve searched through available documentation and tried different approaches, but I haven’t been able to identify the root cause.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or can provide guidance on what additional configuration, role, or restriction I should verify?

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Kind regards,
Octavio

entry.title: sap data services connect starrocks issue
entry.content:
Hello experts:

I need to use  data services to extract data from starrocks database, which is compatiable with mysql. then when creating  the datastore, I chose the mysql type, but it prompted an error :

WARNING: Cannot establish database connection.SQL submitted to database <information_schema> resulted in error<[MySQL][ODBC 8.0(w) Driver][mysqld-8.0.33]Getting analyzing error.Detail message: Set statement only support constant expr..>. The SQLsubmitted is <
SET SESSION sql_mode=(SELECT CONCAT(@@sql_mode,',ANSI_QUOTES'))
>.' (BODI-1111341)

it seems that the starrocks does not support the subquery to assign value.  but the connection is almost successful except the initial submitted sql statement.

I've tried serveral ways to fix it, as following:

1.  change the database type to odbc

2.  input something in the additional session parameters under "advanced"

3. tried to use data services 4.3 to establish the datastore

But so far no progress. it seems that the data services need to sent the initial statement SET SESSION sql_mode=(SELECT CONCAT(@@sql_mode,',ANSI_QUOTES')) ,  don't know how to skip this step.

Could anyone help? Thanks.

 

Allen

entry.title: SF EC/ECP Data transformation with Integration with SAP S4/Hana Public cloud
entry.content:
BACKGROUND

We are currently migrating from on-prem ECC system to SAP SuccessFactors EC/ECP and SAP S4 /HANA Public cloud, we are implementing a coupled go-live of SuccessFactors and S/4HANA. AS per SAP’s limitation in S4/Hana Public cloud we have to use the certain default configurations to define our enterprise structure in public cloud. Because of this we can’t define our ECC controlling area in S4 and have to use the controlling are in S4 as A000.

The answer to the key question we are looking get here is whether data transformation within EC and ECP is required, and what SAP's supported approach is for this scenario.

Data specifics - The data which will be held in Employee Central and Employee Central Payroll — references the original controlling area ( from ECC – to ECP for majority of the data that includes – Past payroll results, cost distributions- table ASSHR and ASSOB,  But the data replication from the EC to ECP – updates only infotype 1 with new controlling area). We understand that when payroll data is posted from ECP to S4, the system performs an automatic conversion(We have tested that- we checked that if you modify any historical records lets say cost distribution then system changes its controlling area of teh record to new one). However, this automatic conversion does not address the historical data held within EC and ECP where we have concerns about:

  1. Retro calculations across two controlling areas: Will SAP support retro payroll calculations within ECP that span two different controlling areas (ECC old  and the new A000) without first transforming the historical records held in EC and ECP?
  2. Data integrity within EC and ECP: If historical data within Employee Central and Employee Central Payroll retains references to the old controlling area post-migration, what are the implications for data integrity within the SuccessFactors environment? Specifically, does SAP consider this a supported and clean configuration for a new system going live?
  1. Payroll posting file reconciliation: During our Parallel Payroll Run we will extract, transform, and load data from ECC into EC and ECP, then run payroll in ECP and post the results to S4. If no data transformation is applied within EC/ECP prior to this process, what risk does it have in terms of accuracy and completeness of the payroll posting file posted to S4?
  1. Audit trail and compliance: If we update historical records in EC and ECP to reflect a new controlling area that did not exist when those records were created, what guidance does SAP provide for maintaining an appropriate audit trail?
  2. SAP's recommended approach: What is SAP's recommended approach for ensuring that EC and ECP data are aligned with the new S4 controlling area structure at go-live? Is the transformation of historical data within EC/ECP a supported, recommended activity, and if so, what tooling or methodology does SAP recommend?

entry.title: SAP Advantage ODBC Drivers CP936 Support
entry.content: Hi. May I ask if SAP Advantage ODBC Drivers supports CodePage 936/Chinese and if yes which version ships with it? I'm currently using version 12.

entry.title: Can not get my Oracle or MSSQL stored procs to drag and drop onto the Data Flow canvas
entry.content:
I use DataService Designer 4.3.3.222

I have created several Oracle and MSSQL SP as described in your manual:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM/aa4cb9ab429349e49678e146f05d7341/459568356e041014910aba7db0e91070.html?version=4.3.5

But it does not allow me to drag and drop the imported SP from Datastore onto Data Flow canvas.

entry.title: Guidance Needed: LMS → Snowflake Data Ingestion & Identifying Correct API Endpoints
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We are currently working on a project to ingest SAP SuccessFactors LMS data into Snowflake for reporting and analytics. We’ve reviewed the available documentation, including the Account Data report reference here:

  1.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/lm/3354618614.html
  2. https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/sap-successfactors-learning-odata-apis/background-knowledge-for-sap-successfactors-learning-web-services
  3. https://me.sap.com/notes

    At this stage, we are able to retrieve the Student table, but we are struggling to identify the correct API endpoints for additional user‑related fields. Many attributes we need (e.g., extended user profile fields, account‑level metadata, etc.) do not appear in the Student table or in the standard PRD/BIRT report definitions.

    Our questions are:

    1. Which LMS API endpoints expose full user information beyond the Student table?

    2. Is there a recommended approach or best practice for extracting complete user data for external ingestion (e.g., Snowflake, data lake, etc.)?

    3. Has anyone successfully implemented an LMS → Snowflake or similar pipeline and can share which endpoints or reports were required?

    4. Are there hidden or less obvious endpoints for user attributes that are not documented in the standard help pages?

    Any guidance, examples, or direction to the correct documentation would be greatly appreciated.

    Thank you!

entry.title: Unable to Create "Custom Analytical Queries and Services" in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi Expert, 

I would like to add a custom query in my starter system, and i can access to this app "Custom Analytical Queries and Services", but unable to do any movement in this app.

I have checked my authorization which Z_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST that based on template role "BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST", and all "unrestricted". But I still cannot edit or create in that query app.

And also check IAM app is active.

Is there any authorization role or catelog I am lacking of?

 

Tina

entry.title: Netweaver and BW compatibility with BODS 2025
entry.content:
BODS DIGITS is compatible with SAP applications that run on supported SAP NetWeaver versions, with 7.5 SP31 being the latest certified version. We are currently planning to upgrade to BODS DIGITS, using SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform. However, our ECC system is running on SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP28.

1) Could you please confirm whether BODS DIGITS (with SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform) will work with ECC system (SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP28)? 

2)Also  Could you please confirm whether BODS DIGITS (with SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform) will work with BW system (750 SP28) 


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Data Services install \ upgrade

Q: What version of Data Services are you installing?
A: Data services DIGITS

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Failed getting banner error in SAP Data Services
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am getting following error when 2-3 or more jobs are executing in parallel which access same SFTP server.

Could not initialize SSH transport layer for file location object <file_location>. Error: <(-43) Failed getting banner>.

Please note that when jobs are executing in serial, they work absolutely fine. Checked with BASIS team and they said it is issue from the FTP infra team related to concurrent session limit.

Checked with FTP infra team for concurrent session limit and they said they are not using sshd daemon for sftp connection to modify concurrent session limit. They are using Crushftp application and there is no limit for concurrent sessions.

Also checked with Firewall team as suggested by FTP infra team and no issues were observed from Firewall side either. I have also kept Connection retry count as 10 and connection retry interval as 10 to delay the failure. Sometimes it works but sometimes it doesn't.

What else can be checked in this scenario.?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: busco parntner para SAP Data Services en Mexico
entry.content: Hi, Soy Edwin Orta empleado del INE, actualmente estamos buscando un partner para la plataforma SAP Data Services que nos pueda apoyar en la gestión de soporte así como la descarga de software y address directories.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA PCE Migration: Datasphere vs. BODS with Azure – Architecture Recommendations
entry.content:
Hi

My organization has begun its SAP S/4HANA PCE migration. As part of our Phase 2 roadmap, we are evaluating between SAP Datasphere and SAP BusinessObjects Data Services (BODS) with Microsoft Azure serving as the data storage layer.

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) will be our enterprise reporting and analytics solution.

FYI, we do not currently have an enterprise data warehouse.

Given this landscape, I would appreciate the community's insights and recommendations on the following topics:

  • Does BODS support extraction of SAP S/4HANA CDS views? If so, are there any limitations or best practices?
  • How well does BODS integrate with Azure-based data storage for SAP data?
  • How do the licensing costs compare between SAP Datasphere (including storage) and a BODS + Azure storage architecture?
  • What capabilities or features would we be giving up by choosing BODS with Azure storage instead of SAP Datasphere?
  • Are there any other architectural, operational, or long-term considerations that we should take into account before making this decision?

Any experiences, recommendations, or lessons learned from similar implementations would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: export data from sap bods using api method
entry.content:
Hello,

using sap bods 4.3, can we export data to external using using api method. source is from sap bw and the external vendor prefers api method. can you share any documentation or steps needed to perform this data export.

entry.title: SAP Data Services - Is it possible to hide source table names, target table names and generated SQL
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently evaluating SAP Data Services logging behavior and would appreciate guidance regarding Job Error Log visibility.

Background
----------

A test job was created to evaluate execution and error logging behavior.

Job:
JB_TRACE_LOG_ANALYSIS

Workflow:
WF_TRACE_ANALYSIS

Dataflow:
DF_LOAD_EMPLOYEE

The purpose of the test was to determine whether internal implementation details can be suppressed from the generated logs while still preserving meaningful error information.

Testing Performed
-----------------

The following trace options were disabled during execution:

- Session
- Workflow
- Dataflow

Results
-------

After disabling these trace options, Trace Log verbosity was reduced as expected.

Workflow and Dataflow related execution details were no longer visible in the Trace Log.

However, during a simulated failure (ORA-00001 Unique Constraint Violation), the Job Error Log still displayed technical implementation details, including:

- Source table names
- Target table names
- Generated SQL statements
- Constraint names
- Database object references

Example:

ORA-00001: unique constraint violated

PK_TGT_EMPLOYEE

INSERT INTO TGT_EMPLOYEE ...

FROM SRC_EMPLOYEE

Requirement
-----------

The objective is to retain only high-level error visibility such as:

- Job Failed
- Error Code
- Error Message

while suppressing technical implementation details such as:

- Source table names
- Target table names
- Generated SQL statements
- Constraint names
- Internal object references

Questions
---------

1. Is there any SAP Data Services configuration, repository setting or Management Console option that masks or suppresses source table names, target table names and generated SQL from Job Error Logs?

2. Are Job Error Logs intentionally designed to always expose database-generated SQL exceptions?

3. Is there any SAP-supported approach to display only high-level error information while hiding implementation details?

4. Has anyone implemented a custom logging or error-handling solution to achieve this requirement?

Any guidance, recommendations or SAP Notes references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.
